## Current Role: Program Repository

This repository — `buck-HCI-AI/HCI_AI_Operating_System` — serves a **dual role**:

1. **Implementation Repository** — Contains live engineering work (WF-007 is live, integrations active, source code in 03_Source_Code)
2. **Program Repository** — Contains governance, architecture, sprint management, and AI operating standards

**Architecture Decision (2026-06-26):** This repository is now transitioning to serve as the HCI AI Program Repository — the permanent coordination and governance layer — while also housing the existing implementation work. No implementation code will be removed. The governance layer and implementation layers coexist in this single repository unless a formal separation directive is issued and approved by @buck-HCI-AI.

## Program Repository Purpose

The Program Repository is the **control system** for all HCI AI work. It defines:

- **Who** does what (AI_TEAM_CHARTER.md, AI_WORKFLOW_ROLES.md)
- **How** work flows (SPRINT_OPERATING_MODEL.md, CONTRIBUTING.md)
- **What** is authorized and what requires approval (APPROVAL_GATES.md, AUTOMATION_GOVERNANCE.md)
- **Where** everything lives (PROGRAM_REPOSITORY_INVENTORY.md, REPOSITORY_RELATIONSHIP_MAP.md)
- **What** the current state is (LIVE_PROJECT_STATE.md — template in progress)
- **What** the roadmap is (PROJECT.md, TASKS.md, milestones)
